title: Founding Designer for the Film Zone
label: Film Zone Experience
description: I worked alongside the founder to execute a systematic and foundational design language that fit the 3 month sprint for launch.
intro: The Film Zone is a social media startup based on film appreciation across platforms. I worked alongside  founder to execute a systematic and foundational design language that fit the 3 month sprint for launch.

sections.heading: The Approach
sections.body: I designed a core flow of interactions, behaviors, and features that defined the next big movie recommendation application. Working alongside development and founder meant quick fixes, broad scope, and big ideas.

sections.heading: The Strategy
sections.body: The strategy for the initial engagement of users was essential — we created a social-first film-watching experience, so that you'll never see another bad movie… unless your friends, curators, and famous people have bad taste.

sections.heading: The Core
sections.body: Like all great ideas, we discovered the core interaction flow by playing. We decided to educate users through the onboarding experience, a light version of the main flow. This way our user knows exactly how to stamp and rate a film, the core element of our product.

items.label: Balancing adoption and intuition
items.body: Watching the onboarding experience, tracking dropoff, and where individuals stop filling out profile and tracking movies.

items.label: Initial engagement
items.body: Testing core features of feed-based curation, as well as saving movies a user intends to watch.

items.label: Social proof and sharing
items.body: Where users invite friends and family to the platform, and where these users follow through or drop off.
